interpretation_summary: The replication closely followed the preregistered plan: same DV (Benefits − Symbols difference), same factors (between: Level/Condition; within: ProductType), and preregistered exclusions. Due to a missing dependency (pingouin), the analysis used a mathematically equivalent fallback (subject-wise difference two-sample t-test with F = t^2) to test the Level × ProductType interaction. The interaction was significant (F≈10.89, p≈0.001), supporting the focal claim relative to the original (F(1,204)=7.70, p=0.006). Outputs were saved and independently verified from generated files.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: High alignment with preregistration: same operationalization (ScoreDiff), same exclusion rules, same 2×2 design and focal interaction test. Minor deviation: mixed ANOVA via pingouin was replaced by an equivalent t-test on subject-wise differences (F = t^2).

deviations.issue_description: pingouin mixed_anova unavailable; used subject-wise difference two-sample t-test to compute the interaction (reported as F = t^2). Main-effect rows were left NA in the output.
deviations.impact: Low for the focal claim (equivalent interaction test; significance unchanged). Moderate for completeness (main effects not estimated).

deviations.issue_description: Cell means by Level within ProductType were not reported in outputs, so directional pattern was not explicitly verified beyond the presence of the interaction.
deviations.impact: Medium: Does not affect the preregistered significance criterion but limits interpretability of effect direction.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: The Level (Category vs. Brand) × Product Type (Utilitarian vs. Symbolic) interaction on the Benefits − Symbols difference score is statistically significant.
results_comparison.replication_results: Equivalent interaction test (subject-difference t leading to F = t^2): F(1,555) = 10.892, p = 0.00103, partial eta-squared = 0.019; N (post-exclusions) = 557.
results_comparison.overall_answer: Yes for the focal claim: the preregistered criterion (significant Level × ProductType interaction) is met.

replication_report: Replication successful: The Level × ProductType interaction on Benefits − Symbols is significant (F(1,555)=10.89, p=0.001), consistent with the original finding (F(1,204)=7.70, p=0.006). Deviation limited to an equivalent statistical fallback due to package availability.

failure_handling.suggestions: Pin and install pingouin (and dependencies) in the Docker image; alternatively implement AnovaRM or mixed-effects models in statsmodels as a primary method and cross-check F with the t-test fallback.

Augment outputs to include full ANOVA (main effects) and cell means/SEs for each ProductType × Level to verify directionality; consider Welch’s t-test or mixed-effects modeling to relax equal-variance assumptions.

notes: The fallback t-test assumes equal variances across Level groups; with large N this is likely minor, but robustness checks (Welch’s t or LME) are advisable. Effect size (np2≈0.019) indicates a small interaction effect; original paper did not report np2 for direct comparison. Recommend reporting cell means to confirm the theorized directional pattern.
